title: Educational Lab Platform Architecture Work
summary: Private educational lab platform work focused on onboarding, stack comprehension, architecture clarity, documentation alignment, and security-conscious review of cloud deployment flows.

logs.title: Merged 10-day onboarding architecture package
logs.details: Added a structured onboarding package that maps the platform across routing, stack responsibilities, frontend-to-backend flow, authentication, lab listing, launch flow, lab execution, backend session lifecycle, remote machine architecture, integrations, cloud infrastructure, infrastructure as code, and production architecture.

logs.title: Opened documentation cleanup and accuracy audit
logs.details: Started a documentation-only cleanup pass to inventory the docs structure, compare documentation claims against the current codebase, update GitHub-readable indexes, clarify local setup around the main application, and separate current implementation reality from older architecture notes.

logs.title: Advanced documentation audit and lab session architecture mapping
logs.details: Reviewed top-level and main technical documentation, converted a generic dependency note into repo-specific contributor guidance, added a current lab session flow diagram, and marked older visual architecture material as partially accurate instead of treating it as source of truth.

logs.title: Paused cloud quick start rewrite pending maintainer direction
logs.details: The deployment/security validation path has now been smoke tested end-to-end, which unblocks continuing the documentation PR and revisiting the cloud quick start against the accepted hardened lifecycle flow instead of stale setup assumptions.

logs.title: Reviewed and hardened cloud lab lifecycle security flow
logs.details: Completed a focused security review, cleanup, and live smoke-test pass around the cloud lab lifecycle boundary. The hardened flow now validates protected lifecycle API access, approved image allowlisting, browser-driven lab launch, managed instance tagging, expiry metadata, cleanup automation, and full stack teardown. Follow-up local developer-experience gaps were tracked separately so they do not block the P0 hardening merge.

logs.title: Mapped creator-mode lab versioning across virtual-machine and container-backed labs
logs.details: Reviewed a proposed creator-mode lab versioning model against cloud container and virtual-machine behavior. The current working abstraction is to open an editable lab environment, modify it, save a draft version, test it, and publish after validation. For the next implementation pass, the practical MVP direction is local Docker editing and building for container-backed labs: edit locally, build a reproducible image, push a versioned artifact, register the runtime definition, and test before publish. Hosted browser workspaces remain a later product direction rather than the immediate PR scope.

nextSteps: Merge the validated security hardening pull request when maintainer review is complete
Continue the documentation PR now that the hardened deployment path has been smoke tested
Start the Save State / Save Version implementation using local Docker editing and reproducible container-image artifacts as the MVP path
Keep larger hardening follow-ups tracked separately: stronger role isolation, proper machine-image capture workflow design, final authorization cache strategy, and local developer-experience cleanup
blockers: Cloud quick start rewrite still depends on maintainer acceptance of the validated deployment path
Older visual diagrams should be retained until maintainers confirm whether to replace, rebuild, or archive them
Container-backed lab versioning still needs implementation validation before it should be documented as final behavior
